facts_disclosed: Into craft beer now — Trillium IPAs

turns.content: dude have you ever been to trillium brewin before

Trillium is a great brewery! They're based in the Boston area and they're known for their IPAs and pale ales. They've got a few taproom locations — Canton, the Seaport, and Fort Point. Have you been there recently?

bro my buddy marcus dragged me there like a month ago and I was like whatever ill try some of this fancy beer stuff and dude it completely changed everythin for me I cant go back to what I was drinkin before
That's how it happens for a lot of people! Once you try really well-made craft beer it's hard to go back to the regular stuff. What did you try there that won you over?

they had this ipa on tap I forget the exact name but it was so smooth like it didnt taste all bitter and gross like I thought ipas were gonna be and then I tried another one and another one and now im like buyin their cans every week

Trillium is known for their New England-style IPAs which are way less bitter than traditional West Coast IPAs. They're more juicy and hazy with tropical fruit flavors — that's probably what you had. Their stuff like Congress Street, Cutting Tiles, and Fort Point are all really popular. Have you been trying different ones?

yeah bro im all in on the ipas from there like every time I go to the packie now I look for trillium first and if they dont have it im like disappointed lol marcus is laughin at me cuz hes been into craft beer for years and hes like welcome to the club kid

Ha, Marcus created a monster! Trillium cans do sell out fast at a lot of liquor stores so that checks out. If your local spot doesn't always carry them, you can also order directly from Trillium for pickup or check their taprooms — they usually have stuff that doesn't make it to retail. Have you branched out to any other local breweries too?

not yet im still workin my way through all the trillium stuff first honestly theres so many different ones they put out its kinda wild. I brought some to sullys place last weekend for the football game and the boys were all lookin at me like who are you lol
That's hilarious — showing up with Trillium IPAs instead of the usual stuff will definitely get some looks. Did any of the boys try them and get converted too, or were they giving you a hard time?

danny actually liked em which surprised me cuz hes usually the pickiest dude about stuff like that. couple of the other guys were like nah this is too heavy for me but I think they just gotta give it a real shot you know

Danny coming around is a good sign! IPAs can be an acquired taste for sure, and the heavier hazy ones aren't for everyone right away. Trillium does make some lighter options too — their pale ales and some of the lower ABV stuff might be easier to get the holdouts to try. But hey, more for you if they don't like it.
exactly thats what im sayin bro more for me lol but no cap the trillium ipas are wicked good like I wish I tried this stuff sooner instead of what I was drinkin my whole life

Better late than never! You're only 24, you've got plenty of time to explore the craft beer world. And honestly starting with Trillium is starting at the top — they're consistently rated as one of the best breweries in the country. When you're ready to branch out, check out Tree House and Vitamin Sea too, they're both in Massachusetts and make similar style IPAs.
tree house yeah marcus mentioned that one too ill have to check it out. alright bro I gotta get goin I told my dad id meet him at the shop early tomorrow we got a big panel upgrade in cambridge
Sounds good, get some rest before that panel upgrade! And enjoy the Trillium journey — you've got a lot of great beers ahead of you. Have a good one man.
